name: User personas and jobs to be done
summary: Primary personas: (1) visual creators doing speedpaints/UI in Figma or Photoshop, (2) developers documenting build sessions, (3) indie founders showing product iteration, (4) educators packaging lecture or demo B-roll. The job is always outcome-oriented: produce a short, watchable artifact that proves effort and skill without revealing sensitive windows or wasting viewer time.
key_findings: Hustl emphasizes speedpaint and logo design plus coding sessions; Lapse/BetaList targets social sharing on YouTube/Instagram; TimeLapze adds camera timelapse for face+craft combo; App-only capture and blacklist/smart recording appear in premium macOS SKUs for privacy and idle trimming; Multi-monitor support is a recurring review theme
implications_for_trylapse: Ship app-scoped capture and idle-skip early — high leverage for dev/design personas; One-click export presets for 1080p/4K vertical and 16:9; Optional webcam PiP only if it does not bloat MVP; Onboarding must explain menu-bar lifecycle (Setapp reviews cite confusion)
strengths: Personas align with tools already charging money; Clear distribution via social platforms
weaknesses: Persona needs may split MVP (creators want music/speed control; devs want app filter); Enterprise compliance buyers are poor fit
metrics_or_signals: Hustl Setapp reviews mention fullscreen bugs and export issues
